_meta.method: Cross-referenced Comunidad Sōtō Zen Colombia / Daishinji (sotozencolombia.org), Montaña de Silencio / Sōfū-ji (montanadesilencio.org), Fundación Zen de Colombia / Templo Gen To (fundacionzen.org), Sangha Bogotá (Plum Village tradition; sangha.bogota@gmail.com), and Buddhistdoor en Español's Colombia directory. Each entry verified against the centre's own site or an official Sōtōshū / Plum Village reference.
_meta.coverageEstimate: Medium (~65-70%) for organised Zen sanghas with a website. Daishinji is the central Sōtōshū-recognised seat for Colombia (proclaimed a Sōtō school temple in South America). Montaña de Silencio in Medellín is a separate active Sōtō community. Plum Village footprint represented by Sangha Bogotá; smaller mindfulness groups in Medellín/Cali/Armenia are documented but lack standalone URLs.
knownGaps: Sangha Bogotá (Plum Village tradition) operates primarily through Facebook/Instagram; no dedicated website
Centro Cultural Bodhidharma (Armenia) and Kasaguadua are listed as Daishinji sub-locations but lack independent URLs
No active Rinzai, Kwan Um, Sanbō Zen International, or Ōbaku presence located as of 2026

places.name: Daishinji — Templo Zen Mente Magnánima
places.lineage: Sōtō (Sōtōshū; Densho Quintero, dharma heir of Shōhaku Okumura Rōshi)
places.notes: Proclaimed a Sōtōshū South America temple. Led by Ven. Densho Quintero (ordained at Antaiji, Japan).

places.name: Montaña de Silencio — Sōfū-ji
places.notes: Main seat of the Montaña de Silencio Sōtō Zen community; daily zazen schedule plus virtual sessions.

places.name: Fundación Zen de Colombia — Templo Gen To 'La Tierra'
places.notes: Rural retreat temple near Cachipay run by monk Reitai Lemort; urban practice groups via fundacion@fundacionzen.org.

places.name: Sangha Bogotá — Tradición Thich Nhat Hanh
places.lineage: Vietnamese Thiền (Plum Village / Thich Nhat Hanh)
places.notes: Active Plum Village sangha in Bogotá; hosted Plum Village monastics during 2023 and 2025 Colombia tours. Contact: sangha.bogota@gmail.com.
